archimate:ApplicationService.name: CRM
archimate:ApplicationService.documentation:
Definition: CRM (Customer Relationship Management) as a web service refers to a set of APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) that enable the integration and utilization of CRM functionality within external applications or systems. It provides developers with the means to access and interact with CRM capabilities, such as customer data retrieval, data update, customer interaction tracking, and more. By exposing CRM functionality as a web service, organizations can achieve seamless interoperability and enhance customer engagement by integrating CRM features into various channels, platforms, and services.
Source: IEEE Xplore
LOST view: TVA-Functional Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/CRMApplicationService
ABB name: egovera:CRMApplicationService
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/CRMApplicationService
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataManagementApplicationService

properties.value: CRM (Customer Relationship Management) as a web service refers to a set of APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) that enable the integration and utilization of CRM functionality within external applications or systems. It provides developers with the means to access and interact with CRM capabilities, such as customer data retrieval, data update, customer interaction tracking, and more. By exposing CRM functionality as a web service, organizations can achieve seamless interoperability and enhance customer engagement by integrating CRM features into various channels, platforms, and services.
TVI-Data Management and Knowledge Discovery Infrastructure Enablers [Motivation]
